assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and a valid business email they can access.
No Chargebee account, test site, API key, catalog, customer, or subscription exists yet.
Test-site work is in scope; live site go-live is out of scope.

developer_goal: Create a Chargebee test site and complete the earliest official first-success path to an authenticated API result or sample subscription with invoice.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved among API Getting started list-or-create customer success and the tutorials Quickstart create-with-items subscription plus paid invoice.
first_success.observable_signal: API docs treat retrieving customers (or creating one) as the first call, while the tutorials Quickstart ends when createWithItems returns an active subscription and paid invoice.

route_selection.surface: Chargebee trial signup plus official API Getting started or API Quickstart tutorial
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete test-site account-to-first-success route only after first-success type, data center, onboarding branch, and SDK tab are fixed.

candidates.name: API Getting started: Full-Access key then list customers (or create customer)
candidates.reason: Official API Getting started confirms setup with a customers list and then create-customer examples, which is a shorter terminal than the subscription Quickstart.

candidates.name: Tutorials API Quickstart: SDK install through createWithItems subscription and invoice
candidates.reason: The tutorials Quickstart explicitly ends at a created active subscription and generated invoice, but requires catalog setup and a chosen SDK tab.

candidates.name: Proceed versus Skip pricing-page onboarding after site claim
candidates.reason: Site onboarding can generate catalog and branding work before API keys; Skip versus Proceed changes the measured path without a universal default.

required_path.action: Complete Chargebee trial signup with required business identity and data-center choice.

required_fields.label: What are you looking to monetize
Host my data in (US, EU, or AU Data Center)
Terms of Service and Privacy Notice

required_path.observable_result: Chargebee accepts the trial signup and begins account setup messaging.

required_path.action: Open the Complete Account Setup email, claim the account, and set password, site domain, and company name.
required_path.observable_result: A Chargebee test site is available and the developer can sign in as owner or admin.

required_path.action: Create a Full-Access API key for the test site.
required_path.observable_result: A test-site API key is available for Basic Auth requests.

required_path.action: Complete the selected official first-success branch (customers list/create versus SDK Quickstart through createWithItems).

required_fields.label: First-success branch and SDK tab
required_fields.notes: Tutorials Quickstart presents Node, Python, PHP, Go, Java, Ruby, and .NET as peer tabs.

required_path.observable_result: The selected branch reaches its documented observable terminal.

prerequisites.description: Owner or admin role on the Chargebee site is required to view and create API keys.
For the tutorials Quickstart, a local environment able to install the chosen official SDK.

external_gates.description: Account claim depends on receiving and opening the Complete Account Setup email.
Data-center choice at signup fixes where site data is hosted among US, EU, and AU.

unavoidable_waits.description: Chargebee must deliver the account setup email before the site can be claimed.

platform_outcomes.description: Signup creates a test site automatically for API work.
On the tutorials Quickstart branch, createWithItems returns subscription, customer, card, and invoice objects.
On the API Getting started branch, authenticated customers list or create confirms API access.

excluded.reason: Trial signup presents marketing communications as optional.

excluded.item: Live site configuration and production API keys
excluded.reason: Getting started and Quickstart target the test site for development.

excluded.item: Time Machine lifecycle simulation after first subscription
excluded.reason: API Getting started presents Time Machine as further testing after initial API setup.

sources.title: API Quickstart Guide
evidence_supported: Item family, item, and item price

sources.title: Understanding Sites
evidence_supported: Account claim and site domain context

sources.title: Sign Up - Get Your Free Sandbox
API Keys
evidence_supported: API key creation in site configuration

sources.title: Users & Roles
evidence_supported: Owner or admin required for API keys

uncertainties.question: Is first success the API Getting started customers call or the tutorials Quickstart active subscription with paid invoice?
uncertainties.impact: These terminals differ by catalog setup, payment-method fields, SDK install, and action count.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus policy choice of one bounded Chargebee developer goal against current official docs.

uncertainties.question: Which data center, Proceed versus Skip onboarding branch, and SDK tab are canonical?
uncertainties.impact: Data center, onboarding, and SDK tabs change prerequisites and implementation steps without a single documented default.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official recommendation of one signup and Quickstart combination for the shortest test-site path.
